summary: Small merged bulk PR touching one stage file and one item file. The stage change cleans up grCastle_801CDFD8 by removing generated-looking temporaries and assigning fields directly. The item change replaces a MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly implementation of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with C that calls it_8026B894 and uses a volatile owner-pointer comparison against ref_gobj.

key_files.role: Stage cleanup in grCastle_801CDFD8; removes extra local temporaries and uses direct grCs_804D6970 and GroundVars castle9 field assignments.
Item decomp change in it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk; removes MWERKS_GEKKO asm/pragmas and NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback, replacing them with C using GET_ITEM, it_8026B894, and a volatile pointer comparison.

details: Removed temporary grCastleParams pointers and scalar locals params, params2, neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value.
Changed random range access to grCs_804D6970->xA directly.
Changed xD4 assignment to grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result directly.
Changed xDC, xDA, xD8, and xD6 assignments to direct literals -1 and 0.
Removed MWERKS_GEKKO guarded inline assembly implementation of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk and the surrounding pragma push/pop.
Removed non-GEKKO NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk.

Added C body that gets Item* ip via GET_ITEM(gobj), calls it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), then takes a volatile pointer to ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and performs a comparison with ref_gobj whose value is discarded.

smart_moves.title: Removed unnecessary temporary locals in stage code
smart_moves.detail: grCastle_801CDFD8 became shorter and more direct while retaining the same visible field writes and HSD_Randi control flow in the diff.

smart_moves.title: Narrowly replaced inline assembly with C for an item callback
smart_moves.detail: The item change removes a hand-written asm block and expresses the known call and owner comparison in C, while using volatile on the owner pointer to preserve an otherwise-unused load/compare shape.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking scalar temporaries can often be collapsed into direct field and literal assignments when the surrounding code does not need the temporaries.

A small inline asm callback may sometimes be expressible in C by recovering the real object type, preserving the real call, and using a narrow volatile access only for the required otherwise-unused memory comparison.

naming_conventions.name: range
naming_conventions.context: Local replacement for random_range in grCastle_801CDFD8.

naming_conventions.name: ip
naming_conventions.context: Local Item* recovered from GET_ITEM(gobj) in it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk; consistent with short role names for clear local object roles.

naming_conventions.name: owner_ptr
naming_conventions.context: Local volatile pointer to ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 used for the preserved comparison against ref_gobj.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.detail: Replaced MWERKS_GEKKO asm for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with C and removed the asm-only pragma wrapper.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Volatile pointer access for unused compare
assembly_or_matching_tactics.detail: The new C takes HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and discards the result of comparing *owner_ptr with ref_gobj, apparently to preserve a load/compare that has no source-level side effect.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR slice provides no objdiff, build, or adjacent-function regression evidence; under loaded standards this should remain a narrow tactic requiring verifier evidence before being generalized.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.detail: grCastle_801CDFD8 removes generated-looking locals for constants and intermediate values, assigning directly into GroundVars fields.

follow_up_queries: PR 2547 Merge multiple PRs grcastle itnessyoyo

notes: PR body is empty and there are no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the loaded slice.
Diff evidence clearly identifies changed files and exact code changes.
No build, objdiff, checkdiff, or regression output is included, so matching success and the necessity of the volatile tactic cannot be independently claimed from this slice.
The title 'Merge multiple PRs' suggests a bulk merge, but the slice only supports two changed files and does not identify the source PRs.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Collapse generated-looking stage temporaries into direct assignments when values are single-use
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grCastle_801CDFD8, the PR removed single-use temporaries for grCs_804D6970 fields, -1, 0, and the final xD4 value, replacing them with direct field and literal assignments.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow volatile pointer compare used while replacing item inline asm
accepted_candidate_records.text: In it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk, the PR replaced inline asm with C and used HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr to force an otherwise-unused comparison of ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 with ref_gobj.
accepted_candidate_records.caveat: No verifier output is included; record as observed tactic, not a general standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution for volatile dummy comparisons used to remove asm
source_update_candidates.text: This PR provides an example where an inline asm block was replaced by C using a very narrow volatile pointer comparison whose result is discarded. If promoted, standards should emphasize that such volatile tactics require objdiff/build evidence and should not be generalized without proof.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already warn that volatile locals and asm exceptions need evidence; this PR is a concrete historical example but lacks verifier output.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the volatile owner_ptr comparison is an accepted global matching pattern; the slice lacks objdiff or review evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent or source-PR identities from the bulk title; no comments or body text are present.
Do not claim a specific function match or regression-free result from this PR slice alone.
